name: ewokscore

metadata.name: ewokscore
metadata.summary: API for graphs and tasks in Ewoks

documentation:
# ewokscore

*ewokscore* provides an API to define workflows and implement tasks in [ewoks](https://ewoks.readthedocs.io/).

## Install

```bash
pip install ewokscore[test]
```

## Test

```bash
pytest --pyargs ewokscore.tests
```

## Getting started

```python
from ewokscore import Task
from ewokscore import execute_graph


# Implement a workflow task
class SumTask(
    Task, input_names=["a"], optional_input_names=["b"], output_names=["result"]
):
    def run(self):
        result = self.inputs.a
        if self.inputs.b:
            result += self.inputs.b
        self.outputs.result = result


# Define a workflow with default inputs
nodes = [
    {
        "id": "task1",
        "task_type": "class",
        "task_identifier": "__main__.SumTask",
        "default_inputs": [{"name": "a", "value": 1}],
    },
    {
        "id": "task2",
        "task_type": "class",
        "task_identifier": "__main__.SumTask",
        "default_inputs": [{"name": "b", "value": 1}],
    },
    {
        "id": "task3",
        "task_type": "class",
        "task_identifier": "__main__.SumTask",
        "default_inputs": [{"name": "b", "value": 1}],
    },
]
links = [
    {
        "source": "task1",
        "target": "task2",
        "data_mapping": [{"source_output": "result", "target_input": "a"}],
    },
    {
        "source": "task2",
        "target": "task3",
        "data_mapping": [{"source_output": "result", "target_input": "a"}],
    },
]
workflow = {"graph": {"id": "testworkflow"}, "nodes": nodes, "links": links}

# Define task inputs
inputs = [{"id": "task1", "name": "a", "value": 10}]

# Execute a workflow (use a proper Ewoks task scheduler in production)
varinfo = {"root_uri": "/tmp/myresults"}  # optionally save all task outputs
result = execute_graph(workflow, varinfo=varinfo, inputs=inputs)
print(result)
```

## Documentation

https://ewokscore.readthedocs.io/

Ppf workflows pass one dictionary around between tasks and this dictionary
gets updates by each task. This dictionary is unpacked into the unexpected
arguments and passed to the method.

---
param execinfo: true
param inputs: dynamic inputs (from other tasks)

param varinfo: '`Variable` constructor arguments'

functions.name: ewokscore.task_discovery._iter_method_tasks

Yields all task methods from the provided module_names. The module_names will be will

functions.name: ewokscore.task_discovery._iter_ppfmethod_tasks
documentation:
Yields all task ppfmethods from the provided module_names. The module_names will be will
imported for discovery.

The difference with regular methods is that ppfmethods are expected to be called `run`. Other method names will be ignored.

classes.name: ewokscore.scripttask.ScriptExecutorTask
documentation:
Task wrapper for a shell command or script.

When the {code}`SCRIPT_ARGUMENT` input variable is not an existing file, it is assumed to be a command.

When it is a file, it is assumed to be executable when

- Windows: it does not have the `".py"` file name extension
- Linux/Mac: the content does not start with a shebang "#!"

When the file is not executable a command is prepended

- {code}`sys.executable` when the file name has the `".py"` extension
- {code}`bash` otherwise

Examples of different types of command line arguments

- **Single-character argument names**:

  > ```bash
  > python -c "print('hello')"
  > ```
  >
  > ```python
  > inputs = {SCRIPT_ARGUMENT: "python", "c": "print('hello')"}
  > ```

- **Arguments without a value**:

  > ```bash
  > ls -a
  > ```
  >
  > ```python
  > inputs = {SCRIPT_ARGUMENT: "ls", "a": ""}
  > ```

- **Multi-character argument names**:

  > ```bash
  > ls --all
  > ```
  >
  > ```python
  > inputs = {SCRIPT_ARGUMENT: "ls", "all": ""}
  > ```

- **Arguments without a name**:

  > ```bash
  > ls .
  > ```
  >
  > ```python
  > inputs = {SCRIPT_ARGUMENT: "ls", 0: "."}
  > ```

- **Merged single-character argument names**:

  > ```bash
  > ls -ltrh
  > ```
  >
  > Since we need `-ltrh` instead of `--ltrh` we can specify
  > it as a positional argument
  >
  > ```python
  > inputs = {SCRIPT_ARGUMENT: "ls", 0: "-ltrh"}
  > ```

methods.name: ewokscore.variable.Variable.copy_without_references
documentation: Copy that does not contain references to uhashable objects

methods.name: ewokscore.variable.Variable._uhash_data
documentation: The runtime value is used.

methods.name: ewokscore.variable.Variable.dump
documentation:
From runtime to persistent value (never overwrite).
Creating the persistent value needs to be atomic.

This silently returns when:
- data persistence is disabled
- already persisted
- data does not have a runtime value (MISSING_DATA)
- non value URI can be constructed

methods.name: ewokscore.variable.Variable.load
documentation:
From persistent to runtime value. This is called when
try to get the value (lazy loading).

This silently returns when:
- data persistence is disabled
- uri is None (i.e. uhash is None)
- raise_error=False

methods.name: ewokscore.variable.Variable.serialize
documentation: Serialize data before persistent storage

methods.name: ewokscore.variable.Variable.deserialize
documentation: Deserialize data after loading from persistent storage

Has a runtime value (python object) and a persistent value (disk or memory).
The location of the persistent value is either provided or derived from the
universal hash of the variable, which itself can be provided or derived from the
variable data.

functions.name: ewokscore.tests.test_examples.assert_convert_graph
documentation:
All graph `representations` need to be known by `convert_graph`. It will always
test the basic representations (e.g. json and yaml) in addition to the provided
`representations`.

The tuple-items in `representations` are: load options, save options, file extension.

functions.name: ewokscore.tests.utils.results.assert_execute_graph_default_result
documentation: The default result is the merged output of all end nodes.

functions.name: ewokscore.tests.utils.results._assert_execute_graph_tasks
documentation:
Check the output of `execute_graph` for each node. When a task is not in `execute_graph_result`,
it will be instantiated.

An expected value can be:
    * `None`: task is not executed and therefore does not appear in the results
    * `MISSING_DATA`: task has no output and is therefore cannot be persisted
    * else: task is executed and has output

functions.name: ewokscore.tests.utils.results.assert_execute_graph_values
documentation: Check the output of `execute_graph` for the selected outputs of the selected nodes.

functions.name: ewokscore.cliutils.utils.parse_parameter
documentation: The format of `input_item` is `"[NODE]:name=value"`

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.graph_io.update_default_inputs
documentation:
Input items have the following keys:

- name: input variable name
- value: input variable value
- id (optional): node id
- label (optional): used when `id` is missing
- task_identifier (optional): used when `id` is missing
- all (optional): used when `id`, `label` and `task_identifier` are missing (`True`: all nodes, `False`: start nodes)

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.graph_io.parse_inputs
documentation:
Input items have the following keys:

- name: input variable name
- value: input variable value
- id (optional): node id
- label (optional): used when `id` is missing
- task_identifier (optional): used when `id` is missing
- all (optional): used when `id`, `label` and `task_identifier` are missing (`True`: all nodes, `False`: start nodes)

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.graph_io.parse_outputs
documentation:
Output items have the following keys:

- name (optional): output variable name (all outputs when missing)
- new_name (optional): optional renaming when `name` is defined
- id (optional): node id
- label (optional): used when `id` is missing
- task_identifier (optional): used when `id` is missing
- all (optional): used when `id`, `label` and `task_identifier` are missing (`True`: all nodes, `False`: end nodes)

- new_name (optional): optional renaming when name is defined

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.graph_io.add_output_values

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.subgraph._get_subnode_attributes
documentation: Update all input node attributes of the subgraph with the graph node attributes from the super graph

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.subgraph.extract_graph_nodes
documentation:
Removes all graph nodes from `graph` and returns a list of edges
between the nodes from `graph` and `subgraphs`.

Nodes in sub-graphs are defines in the `link_attrs_subgraph_keys` link attribute.
For example:

% code: python
%
% link_attrs = {
%     "source": "subgraph1",
%     "target": "subgraph2",
%     "data_mapping": [{"source_output":"return_value", "target_input": 0}],
%     "link_attrs_subgraph_keys": {
%         "sub_source": ("subsubgraph", ("subsubsubgraph", "task2")),
%         "sub_target": "task1",
%     },
% }

variables.name: ewokscore.graph.subgraph.NodeIdType
documentation: Any is NodeIdType

classes.name: ewokscore.graph.taskgraph.TaskGraph
documentation:
The API for graph analysis is provided by `networkx`.
Any directed graph is supported (cyclic or acyclic).

Loop over the dependencies of a task

```python
for source in taskgraph.predecessors(target):
    link_attrs = taskgraph.graph[source][target]
```

Loop over the tasks dependent on a task

```python
for target in taskgraph.successors(source):
    link_attrs = taskgraph.graph[source][target]
```

For acyclic graphs, sequential task execution can be done like this:

```python
taskgraph.execute()
```

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.multigraph.flatten_multigraph
documentation: The attributes of links between the same two nodes are merged.

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.execute.sequential.instantiate_task

Default input from the persistent representation are added internally.

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.execute.sequential.instantiate_task_static
documentation:
Instantiate destination task while no access to the dynamic inputs.
Side effect: `tasks` will contain all predecessors.

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.execute.sequential.execute_graph
documentation:
Sequential execution of DAGs.

When `output_tasks` is `True` the arguments `outputs` and `merge_outputs`
are ignored and instead of returning output values, it returns `Task` instances.
This was introduced for testing.

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.serialize._find_graph_path
documentation:
When the {code}`path` is relative, the parent directory is assumed to be
(in order of priority):

- {code}`root_dir`
- {code}`root_module` directory
- current working directory

When {code}`path` is not found it tries to find the path with a different
extension from {code}`possible_extensions`.

```
param path

could be a relative path, might have no extension

param root_dir

in case  is relative

param root_module

in case  is not provided

param possible_extensions

in case  is not found

raises

FileNotFoundError
```

functions.name: ewokscore.graph.analysis._iter_nodes
documentation:
Recursion is not stopped by the node or link filters.
Recursion is stopped by either not having any successors/predecessors
or encountering a node that has been visited already.
The original node on which we start iterating is never yielded.

documentation: Send an ewoks start event on enter and stop event on exit.

methods.name: ewokscore.task.Task._send_event
documentation: Send an ewoks event

methods.name: ewokscore.task.Task.cleanup_references
documentation:
Removes all references to the inputs.
Side effect: fixes the uhash of the task and outputs

methods.name: ewokscore.task.Task.run
documentation: To be implemented by the derived classes

methods.name: ewokscore.task.Task.cancel
documentation:
Function called when a task is cancelled.
To be implemented by the derived classes

Node in a task Graph with named inputs and outputs.

The universal hash of the task is equal to the universal
hash of the output. The universal hash of the output is
equal to the hash of the inputs and the task nonce.

A task is done when its output exists.

This is an abstract class. Instantiating a `Task` should be
done with `ewokscore.inittask.instantiate_task`.

functions.name: ewokscore.hashing.uhash
documentation: Universial hash (as opposed to python's `hash`).

classes.name: ewokscore.hashing.UniversalHashable

methods.name: ewokscore.hashing.UniversalHashable.fix_uhash
documentation: Fix the uhash when it is derived from the uhash data.

methods.name: ewokscore.hashing.UniversalHashable.cleanup_references
documentation:
Remove all references to other hashables.
Side effect: fixes the uhash when it depends on another hashable.

The universal hash of an instance of this class is based on:

 * pre-uhash
 * instance nonce (if any)

The universal hash is equal to the pre-hash when an instance nonce is not provided.

The pre-uhash is either provided or based on:

 * data
 * class nonce (class qualifier name, class version, superclass nonce)
